message:
perf(fcp-audit): share canonical ids across verify_chain_with_signers (br-atd32)

Closes flywheel_connectors-atd32 (PERF/P2).

Profiling found that verify_chain_with_signers() paid ~2 full
canonical-CBOR + BLAKE3 passes per entry:
  1. For each entry: verify_signature() -> signing_bytes() ->
     computed_id() -> canonical_cbor(AuditEntryIdMaterial) + blake3.
  2. Then verify_chain() on the same chain recomputed computed_id()
     for every entry again to check the audit.object_id_mismatch
     invariant and build the linkage table.
On large chains or fat metadata payloads this doubled the hot-path
serialization cost before any head checks even ran.

Fix: canonicalize each entry once, thread the result through both the
signature check and the chain-integrity check.

New public API (purely additive):
  * AuditEntry::signing_bytes_from_id(id: &str) -> Vec<u8>
      Infallible: builds the signing transcript from a pre-computed
      canonical id. Same bytes as signing_bytes() for a well-formed
      entry; guaranteed by the new regression test.
  * AuditEntry::verify_signature_with_id(key, id) -> Result<(), _>
      Consumes signing_bytes_from_id internally, skipping the
      redundant computed_id() call inside the legacy verify_signature.
  * verify_chain_with_precomputed_ids(entries, head, zone, ids)
      The original verify_chain body, parameterized over a slice of
      Result<String, AuditError> for the canonical ids. verify_chain
      now computes the slice once and delegates.

verify_chain_with_signers() now:
  1. Computes precomputed_ids once.
  2. Iterates entries zipped with precomputed_ids, calling
     verify_signature_with_id(&key, &id) on each — no per-entry
     canonicalize.
  3. Calls verify_chain_with_precomputed_ids with the same slice.

Net: 2 full canonicalizations per entry → 1. The inline
computed_id() path and every other non-signer verify_chain caller
are byte-identical to before.

Regression tests:
  * signing_bytes_from_id_matches_signing_bytes_for_signed_entry
    — asserts byte-identical transcripts and that both verify paths
    accept the legitimate signature + reject a wrong key. Pins the
    perf fast path against accidental transcript drift.
  * verify_chain_with_precomputed_ids_matches_verify_chain
    — runs both paths over a 4-entry signed chain and asserts the
    resulting VerifyReport (status / chain_len / issues / zone) is
    identical. Pins the refactor.

AuditError already derives Clone, so feeding the precomputed
Result<String, AuditError> through both loops requires no changes
to the error type.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-audit — 382 pass (380 baseline + 2 new),
zero failures.

Cod_4 / SunnyMoose
